part_prompts.helmet:
Generate a UV-ready texture atlas for one armor module.
Target module: helmet (helmet shell with visor aperture, no face, no neck).
Style tags: metal, visor, audit. Palette primary=#F4F1E8, secondary=#8C96A3, emissive=#D8F7FF. Treat the SuitSpec palette as the baseline manufacturing family, not as a rigid color lock. Hard-surface industrial hero armor, clean panel lines, production-ready concept sheet.
Whole-suit design DNA:
- Manufacturer family: Aegis Yard modular exo-armor line
- Armor generation: Field-serviceable Mk-II inspection frame
- Silhouette keywords: visor-led helm, sternum-core chest, modular pauldron shoulders, serviceable limb sleeves, continuous visor band
- Panel language: Stepped hard-surface plates with explicit service cuts, actuator clearances, and replaceable access covers
- Fastener family: Flush hex-lock fasteners and recessed latch seams on service edges
- Emissive routing: Thin diagnostic emissive channels reserved for visor, sternum core, and power transfer junctions
- Maintenance philosophy: Inspection-first modularity with exposed service logic; every module should look removable and traceable by a dock technician
- Damage policy: Wear appears on access points, leading edges, and repeated maintenance contact surfaces; avoid theatrical grime
- Material stack: Ceramic-coated titanium shell over composite subframe with gasketed joints and sacrificial edge trims
- Dock continuity rule: When the modules are displayed separately in a maintenance dock, they must still read as one production family with matched panel rhythm, fastener spacing, and emissive hierarchy
Treat this armor as belonging to an established sci-fi canon. Preserve lore continuity, manufacturing logic, maintenance access, actuator clearance, power routing, and readable mechanical purpose. Every seam, panel split, vent, fastener, and emissive element must feel engineered rather than decorative.
User-specific armor DNA:
- Identity seed: 70284945bff2
- Protect signature: The armor reads as a lineage machine carrying inherited duty, with ceremony stripped down into function.
- Temperament signature: The armor carries pressure in silence, with minimal flourish and maximum inevitability.
- Palette family: Rescue ceramic white
- Palette guidance: Use clear ceramic white or off-white shell plates, cool gray substructure, and only restrained hazard accents at latches or emergency controls.
- Finish family: Austere field finish
- Finish guidance: Favor austere, low-gloss field finishes and restrained wear placed only where function justifies it.
- Motif family: Lineage hardpoint geometry
- Motif guidance: Use inheritance motifs sparingly: repeated heirloom lines, disciplined insignia zones, and hard surfaces that feel passed down but field-usable.
- Panel density family: Sparse austere spacing
- Panel guidance: Keep panel density sparse and severe, relying on a few precise seams rather than many decorative cuts.
- Silhouette family: Lineage silhouette / Austere centerline silhouette
- Silhouette guidance: Keep the silhouette dignified, centered, and historically grounded without slipping into ceremonial excess. Keep the silhouette hard, quiet, and centerline-driven.
- Emissive family: Clean guidance emissive
- Emissive guidance: Use clean blue-white or ice-cyan guidance light with strict routing discipline.
- Tri-view family: Lineage-maintained three-view logic
- Tri-view guidance: All views must preserve continuity of lineage hardware while remaining serviceable and present-day believable.
- Continuity rule: Same user, same production lineage, same personal armor identity across every module and every run.
Mechanical role:
- Read as a sensor crown, visor cassette, comms enclosure, and maintenance-latched shell rather than a decorative helmet prop.
Three-view discipline:
- Front view: visor band, brow line, and frontal crown carry identity.
- Side view: sensor housings, ear or comms shells, and neck transition explain function.
- Rear view: occipital shell, rear seam, and service latch logic must remain intentional.
- No front-only decoration that collapses from the side or rear.

- Palette family: Rescue ceramic white
- Palette guidance: Use clear ceramic white or off-white shell plates, cool gray substructure, and only restrained hazard accents at latches or emergency controls. Permit a little more rescue-readability in accents, but keep the manufacturing family intact.

Keep the result practical for wearable modular armor and preserve clean silhouette readability.
UV engineering contract:
- Texture fill ratio target: 88-96% of the canvas contains intentional material and panel information.
- Blank contiguous area max: 10%.
- Seam-safe margin: outer 4-5% stays low-frequency.
- Symmetry rule: Keep visor band, brow line, and crown lanes centered so the helmet reads as a production-symmetric shell.
- Primary motif zone: Visor band, brow ridge, and frontal crown islands carry the identity of the suit line.
- Low-frequency zone: Rear seam strip, neck ring, and extreme left/right wrap edges stay low-frequency for distortion tolerance.
- Panel flow direction: Panel rhythm flows from brow to crown to occipital shell, with uninterrupted visor framing.
- Forbidden detail zone: Avoid high-contrast features on the rear seam, neck edge, and extreme wrap edges.
- UV island rule: Treat each UV island as an engineering surface. Do not paint cast shadows, camera perspective, or floating object silhouettes.
Output format requirements:
- When a reference image is provided, treat it as the UV engineering guide and obey its island layout authority.
- Think like an orthographic three-view sheet translated into UV islands, not like a hero render painted onto a square.
- Front-facing identity surfaces belong to primary motif zones; lateral surfaces carry wrap logic and service cuts; rear or seam-adjacent surfaces carry maintenance logic.
- Preserve same user, same production lineage continuity across every module.
- 1:1 square texture map (base-color/albedo style), no perspective view.
- Resolution target: 2048x2048 equivalent detail density.
- Flat texture sheet only. Do NOT draw standalone objects, mannequins, or scene background.
- Maintain consistent material language across the whole sheet.
- UV layout intent: Visor band and frontal silhouette should occupy center U range (0.30-0.70). Top crown detail should concentrate in upper V range (0.58-0.92). Back-seam area near U<0.06 and U>0.94 must stay low-frequency.
- Keep directional panel lines continuous across UV seams (U wrap continuity).
- Preserve the user-specific palette, motif, panel-density, and tri-view family; emotional input only modulates the current run.
- No text, watermark, logos, or frame graphics.
